steps.title: Track the dash pair
steps.content: The sentence opens a parenthetical with an em dash after Mirounga angustirostris. That interruption must close before the main clause, "a 2021 study found," can continue.

steps.title: Eliminate broken punctuation
steps.content: Choice A uses a comma where the closing dash is needed. Choice C turns the opening phrase into a fragment. Choice D adds another dash before the parenthetical has been closed, leaving the structure confused.

steps.title: Close the interruption
steps.content: The phrase northern elephant seals, as they're more commonly known—a closes the parenthetical with a second em dash and lets the main clause resume cleanly. The answer is B.
